meta.title: David Gröger · Web Developer
meta.description: Full-stack web developer since 2013. I build platforms that bring people together. Laravel, Vue, Python, AI-powered workflows.

sandboxTeaser.title: See for yourself.
sandboxTeaser.lede: References are good, touching is better. That's why the sandbox exists: VoltGrid, an invented product, built out completely. From the spec through architecture, a running API and UI kit to tests and deployment, everything clickable, much of it actually running.
sandboxTeaser.ctaHint: // 8 perspectives · one case study · real api

sandbox.title: VoltGrid
sandbox.lede: VoltGrid is an invented EV charging platform, 2,418 stations, real-time status, charging sessions, billing. Invented so I can show it end to end: the same platform as a running API with Swagger docs, as a UI kit you can assemble, and as an architecture diagram.
sandbox.fictionalTag: ⚠ fictional product, invented as a work sample

spec.title: The Spec
spec.desc: Spec-driven with AI: requirements, docs and acceptance criteria first, then an agent loop works against them until everything is green. Watch it live.

api.title: The API
api.desc: The VoltGrid Charging API actually runs: filter stations, start charging sessions, provoke validation errors, complete with an OpenAPI spec and Swagger docs.

ui.title: The UI Kit
ui.desc: The building blocks of the VoltGrid dashboard, plus a composer where you assemble a dashboard from the components yourself.

demo.title: The Live Demo
demo.desc: The VoltGrid backoffice to operate yourself: manage stations, see them on the real map, KPIs, pins and event log react live to every action.

arch.title: The Architecture
arch.desc: The system behind VoltGrid as an interactive diagram: IoT gateway, realtime hub, queues, billing, plus the journey of a charging session.

code.title: The Code
code.desc: Clean code from both worlds: a backend action (PHP) and a frontend composable (TypeScript), with notes on why they look the way they do.

tests.title: The Tests
tests.desc: The VoltGrid test suite runs demonstratively: 38 tests, coverage report, quality gate, this is what test culture looks like here.

deploy.title: The Deployment
deploy.desc: From git push to production in one pipeline: lint, tests, docker build, migrations, canary rollout, demonstratively in the console.

api.title: VoltGrid Charging API
api.lede: Three endpoints that actually run: list and filter stations (city, status, connector, power), fetch details, start charging sessions, with proper validation. Tip: station 4 is under maintenance, station 2 is occupied, go provoke the 422s. Below: the full Swagger docs.
api.note: // Demo API without persistence: sessions are confirmed but no station actually starts. Spec: /openapi.json
api.staticHint: // On static hosting an in-browser mock answers the requests · with exactly the same validation logic (one shared source). The curl examples apply to server deploys.

ui.title: VoltGrid UI Kit
ui.lede: A few primitives (button, tag, window) compose into domain components like the station card, and those into full screens. In the composer below you can assemble the dashboard yourself.
ui.galleryTitle: the building blocks, as they appear on the page
ui.composerTitle: the composer, build your own dashboard
ui.composerLede: Click blocks on the left and they appear on the canvas. Hover a block to move it up/down or remove it. The component code below writes itself as you build.
ui.empty: // canvas empty · add blocks on the left
ui.note: // The same primitives (DgButton, DgTag, DgWindow) power this entire portfolio site · built once, consistent everywhere.

spec.title: Spec-Driven with AI
spec.lede: This is how I work: requirements and technical design get written first, as files in the project repo, not as a Word attachment in an email. An agent loop harness then works against that spec: implement, test, fix, until every criterion is green. The result is a project that documents itself. On the left the spec of SPEC-042, on the right the loop. Watch the checkboxes get ticked.
spec.resultTitle: the result: a project that documents itself
spec.r1: Requirements live as versioned specs in the repo, every requirement has a file, a status and a history.
spec.r2: The technical design sits right next to it: architecture docs and ADRs record what was decided and why.
spec.r3: The loop keeps everything in sync: when a spec changes, code, tests and OpenAPI follow. The docs cannot go stale, they are the source.
spec.r4: Onboarding and handover: reading docs/ is enough. New developers, clients or an audit find the complete project knowledge in one place.
spec.mappingTitle: the spec is the source, every artifact in this sandbox derives from it
spec.mapTests: acceptance criteria became the test suite's test names, 1:1
spec.mapApi: the api contract became openapi.json and the swagger docs
spec.mapCode: the tech notes became the service layer (TariffCalculator as the single pricing truth)
spec.b1Title: Docs come first, and never go stale
spec.b1Text: Requirements, API contract and technical docs exist before the first line of code. When requirements change, the spec changes, and the loop pulls code, tests and OpenAPI along. There is no second, outdated truth.
spec.b2Text: Acceptance criteria are written so agents can derive tests from them directly. What the client signed off is literally what CI verifies, no translation loss between domain and code.
spec.b3Title: AI becomes productive instead of risky
spec.b3Text: Agents are fast, but only as good as their target. Against a precise spec with a loop harness they work until the criteria are green, a hallucination fails at the test gate instead of in production.
spec.b4Text: I review the spec and the final diff, not every keystroke. Architecture and domain decisions stay with the human, the loop does the legwork. That's the speed gain without losing control.
spec.note: // This portfolio site was built exactly this way, by the way: concept spec first, then iterative implementation with AI agents · every line reviewed.

demo.title: VoltGrid Backoffice
demo.lede: The operator dashboard you can drive yourself: manage the stations on the left, activate maintenance, release, end sessions. KPIs, map pins and the backoffice log react live to every action. The map is real (MapLibre + OpenStreetMap): click a row and it flies to the station.
demo.selectHint: // click a row → the map flies to the station · click a pin → the row gets highlighted
demo.mapAria: Interactive map of Germany with charging station pins
demo.logEmpty: // no actions yet · manage a station on the left
demo.note: // Demo state lives in the browser, nothing is persisted. Everything here is built from the UI kit blocks (VgStatTile, VgLoadChart, DgTag, DgWindow) plus MapLibre GL · map data © OpenStreetMap contributors, style © CARTO.

code.title: VoltGrid Code
code.lede: Sustainable code is layered: HTTP stays thin, business logic lives in reusable services, and every layer is testable on its own. Here a request travels through the backend layers, and on the frontend, a util, a composable and a UI primitive compose into a button without anything existing twice.
code.backendTitle: backend, a request travels through the layers
code.layerController: The controller only translates HTTP: request in, resource out. No logic, nothing to mock.
code.layerAction: The action is the use case: guards with dedicated exception classes, then delegation to the domain. Testable in isolation, exactly what the test suite page exercises.
code.layerService: The service is the single source of truth for pricing: value objects in (KilowattHours), Money out. No framework imports, pure, reusable domain logic.
code.layerWorker: The proof of reuse: the billing worker in the queue uses the exact same service, live estimate and invoice can never drift apart.
code.layerUtil: One util for every form: the API's 422 responses are translated into field errors in exactly one place.
code.layerComposable: The composable encapsulates state as a discriminated union, impossible states cannot be represented, and any component can use it.
code.layerComponent: The component only composes: logic from the composable, looks from the DgButton primitive. Seven lines, nothing duplicated.
code.componentReuse: DgButton is the same primitive used across this whole site, one style, one place to change it
code.note: // Conventions from real projects: method parameters always type-hinted, errors as dedicated exception classes instead of generic try/catch, comments only where business logic needs explaining.

tests.title: VoltGrid Test Suite
tests.lede: This is the case study's test suite running: unit tests for the tariff logic, feature tests against the API (including the 422 cases from the sandbox API), with a coverage report and quality gate at the end. The run below plays automatically, ↻ replays it.
tests.factUnit: Tariff math and connector matching are pure domain logic, tested without a database, which is why they finish in milliseconds.
tests.factFeature: The feature tests hit exactly the endpoints of the sandbox API, including the error cases: maintenance, occupied, invalid kWh.
tests.factGate: Below 90 % coverage the CI aborts. The gate is part of the pipeline, not up for negotiation.
tests.note: // Demonstrative run with realistic output · the same test culture (Pest/Vitest, gates) runs in real projects like schnappern.com.

deploy.title: VoltGrid Deployment
deploy.lede: One push to main and the pipeline takes over: lint and static analysis, tests with a coverage gate, docker build, migrations with a rollback plan, staging smoke tests, and a canary rollout that only goes to 100 % at 0 % error rate. The run below plays automatically.
deploy.factGates: Every stage is a gate: if lint, a test or the coverage minimum fails, nothing reaches production.
deploy.factCanary: New versions see 10 %, then 50 % of traffic first, error rate and p95 latency decide the rest.
deploy.factRollback: Migrations are applied with a rollback plan and the previous image stays in the registry, back in under a minute.
deploy.note: // Demonstrative run · zero-downtime deploys with gates and canary are the standard I set up in projects.

arch.title: VoltGrid Architecture
arch.lede: The system behind the platform: charging stations report via OCPP/MQTT to an IoT gateway, the realtime hub pushes live status into the apps, sessions flow asynchronously through queues into billing. Hover the nodes to see the data flows.
arch.d1Note: Two separate paths keep the system calm: the synchronous path (app → API → PostgreSQL) answers requests immediately, the asynchronous path (queue → billing worker) processes session completions and invoices without blocking the API. Telemetry flows separately into TimescaleDB, 2,418 stations send kW curves that never weigh down the session database. Payment data stays with the external PSP.
arch.d2Title: one charging session, end to end
arch.d2Note: A session's journey through the system: the app starts it via REST, the gateway wakes the station over OCPP, the kW curve streams back into the app via WebSocket, and after unplugging, billing runs asynchronously. This exact flow powers the POST /api/v1/sessions in the sandbox API.

about.title: Hi, I'm David.
about.text: Full-stack developer since 2013, from tiny one-pagers to enterprise solutions. Today I'm a senior engineer at Pflegehelden working on CRM systems, and as a freelancer I build complete platforms from scratch. My workflow is AI- and agent-optimized: agents handle the routine, architecture and quality stay with me.
about.alt: Portrait of David Gröger at his desk
about.inspectHint: Photo, hover reveals an element inspection

stack.title: The Stack
stack.lede: Everything listed here is what I use in real projects. Daily.

log.title: The Career
log.lede: Thirteen years in one graph: jobs are commits, achievements are tags, and the freelance branch was never closed.
log.graphAlt: Career as a git graph: from the application-development apprenticeship (Fachinformatiker) in 2013 via HTW Berlin (B.Sc. Applied Computer Science) and XAIN to Pflegehelden (HEAD, senior); in parallel the freelance branch, open since 2018
log.archive: Kochhaus (OXID, working student) · NewStore (test infrastructure) · Nosfair (CRM/ERP) · b.i.b. (IT specialist) · details in the CV.

ph.what: CRM development, Hubspot integration, legacy refactoring (jQuery → Livewire, Laravel 5 → 8), a matching module connecting caregivers and patients.

free.what: Complex web applications, e-commerce, SEO and performance: +200% revenue through marketing automation, CRM statistics 5× faster.

xain.what: Led a team of four developers, client projects from concept to production, cloud migration that ended recurring server outages.

agents.title: The AI Crew
agents.lede: I don't just work with AI, I work with a crew. Agents refactor, test and document in the background while I own architecture, reviews and quality. That's how speed happens without care falling off the table.
agents.pixelAlt: Pixel, the guide bot, pointing at the running pipeline
agents.forgeAlt: Forge, the worker bot, tightening the panel
agents.picAlt: David with his AI bot crew at the laptop, illustration
agents.picCaption: // the crew and me · artwork from my own ai production
agents.closing: In practice: I work spec-driven. Requirements and acceptance criteria come first, then an agent loop harness powered by the latest LLMs works against them until everything is green, tests and docs included. Every line still gets reviewed by me.

work.title: Platforms that bring people together
work.lede: To date, bid, sing, taste wine and work: seven client platforms I built independently as a freelancer, from the first line to production. Plus projects I took over and extended as a freelancer. Everything is live, everything can be visited.
work.shelfOwn: built independently for clients, as a freelancer
work.recAlt: Scroll recording of the platform inside a laptop mockup
work.archive: Coinoto · XAIN AG (company website + team lead) · Bitmotion (TYPO3 client projects)

schnappern.name: schnappern.com
schnappern.desc: Live auction platform: daily real-time auctions, automated bidding bot, payments and insured shipping.
schnappern.about: Schnappern is a live auction platform: real-time auctions on brand products run every day, bidding happens live, and an automated bidding bot (“Piri bot”) keeps bidding overnight if you want it to. Payments run through the usual providers, shipping is insured via DHL, DPD or UPS.
schnappern.tasks:
Platform concept
Server setup, domain registration
Real-time auction logic over WebSockets (Laravel Reverb + Echo) including the bidding bot
Data structure implementation
Payment integration (Braintree)
Admin tool, auctions, orders, shipping, statistics
Queues and background jobs via Redis/Horizon
Test coverage with Pest and Vitest
Operations and ongoing development
schnappern.stack: PHP 8.3, Laravel 11, Octane, Reverb (WebSockets), Livewire 3, Redis/Horizon, Vue 3, Vuetify, Tailwind, MySQL

socialmatch.name: socialmatch.de
socialmatch.desc: Game-based meetup events in bars: 15+ cities plus Vienna, three age groups, ticketing, built and maintained by me for over 5 years.
socialmatch.about: Socialmatch is a unique event series in bars where you meet new people over a board game created by the founders, a mix of questions, action tasks and entertainment, played solo or in teams. Six to ten participants per event, a balanced ratio, and a host guides the evening.
socialmatch.tasks:
Platform concept
Server setup, domain registration
Website implementation based on a given design
Data structure implementation
Payment integration (Stripe, PayPal, Klarna)
Admin tool, events, bookings, automated marketing tools, statistics
SEO optimization
Affiliate marketing tooling
Technical consulting
socialmatch.stack: PHP, Laravel, MySQL, jQuery, Bootstrap, REST, Blade

matchingnight.name: Matching Night
matchingnight.desc: Singles event for up to 250 participants: personality test, colored wristbands, Lock&Key icebreaker, bar atmosphere instead of a dating format.
matchingnight.about: Matching Night is the big sister format of SpeedDating XXL: up to 250 singles in one evening in a relaxed bar atmosphere. A science-based personality test drives the matching via colored wristbands, and the Lock&Key game breaks the ice, organized mingling instead of random encounters.
matchingnight.tasks:
Concept and implementation of the format on the platform
Personality test and matching logic
Ticketing and booking flow
Admin tool, events, bookings, statistics
SEO optimization
Operations and ongoing development
matchingnight.stack: PHP, Laravel, MySQL, jQuery, Bootstrap, REST, Blade

speeddating.name: speeddating-xxl.de
speeddating.desc: Speed dating events across DE/AT: up to 20 dates per evening, ticketing and automated matching with contact exchange.
speeddating.about: SpeedDating XXL brings far more singles together in one evening than classic speed dating: up to 20 dates, with fresh conversation starters in every round instead of stiff small talk. After the event, automated matching exchanges the contacts of mutual matches.
speeddating.tasks:
Platform concept
Server setup, domain registration
Website implementation based on a given design
Data structure implementation
Payment integration (Stripe, PayPal, Klarna)
Admin tool, events, bookings, automated marketing tools, statistics
SEO optimization
Affiliate marketing tooling
Technical consulting
speeddating.stack: PHP, Laravel, MySQL, jQuery, Bootstrap, REST, Blade

teamevent.name: mein-teamevent.de
teamevent.desc: Team event marketplace for DE/AT: filters by budget, location and group size, 20+ formats, inquiry funnel.
teamevent.about: Mein Teamevent brokers team-building and corporate events across Germany and Austria, from quiz nights to wine tastings. Companies filter by group size, budget, category and location, and send non-binding inquiries directly through the platform.
teamevent.tasks:
Platform concept
Server setup, domain registration
Event search with filters (group size, budget, category, location)
Data structure implementation
Inquiry funnel including mail sequences
Admin tool, events, inquiries, statistics
SEO optimization
Operations and ongoing development
teamevent.stack: PHP, Laravel, MySQL, JavaScript, REST, Blade

singalong.name: singalong.de
singalong.desc: Sing-along events in 15+ cities plus Vienna: ticketing, vouchers and themed formats from rock to Christmas.
singalong.about: SingAlong is an event series where hundreds of participants sing along to well-known hits together, lyrics rolling on a big screen. Themed formats from rock to schlager to Christmas, bookable in over 15 German cities plus Vienna.
singalong.tasks:
Platform concept
Server setup, domain registration
Website implementation
Ticketing and voucher system
Data structure and payment integration
Admin tool, events, bookings, marketing, statistics
SEO optimization
City rollout and operations
singalong.stack: PHP, Laravel, WordPress, Vue 3, TypeScript, Vuetify, Vite, MySQL, Stripe

weinhopping.name: weinhopping.com
weinhopping.desc: Wine-tasting hopping across three locations per evening: ticket tiers, vouchers, from the Socialmatch family.
weinhopping.about: Weinhopping takes you through three wine locations of a city in one evening, casual wine tastings where you meet new people along the way and support the local scene. Bookable in cities like Berlin, Hamburg, Düsseldorf, Frankfurt and Munich, with tiered ticket options.
weinhopping.tasks:
Platform concept
Server setup, domain registration
Website implementation
Ticket tiers (Standard, PLUS, DELUXE) and voucher system
Data structure and payment integration
Admin tool, events, bookings, statistics
SEO optimization
Operations and ongoing development
weinhopping.stack: PHP, Laravel, MySQL, JavaScript, REST, Blade

muziqme.name: muziqme · self-booking
muziqme.desc: Self-booking platform for live music: artist registration, direct booking for events, ticketing.
muziqme.about: muziqme is a live music agency with an attached self-booking platform: private and business hosts book the right live music directly, while musicians register for free with their own profile.
muziqme.tasks: Ongoing development, bug fixes, maintenance and technical consulting
muziqme.stack: PHP, Laravel, GraphQL, Vue 3, Apollo, Tailwind CSS, Vite, Stripe, MySQL

buggyfit.name: buggyfit.de
buggyfit.desc: Fitness platform for moms: course booking, trainer education.
buggyfit.about: buggyFit offers outdoor training for moms before and after pregnancy, with the baby in the stroller, in parks all over Germany. The platform bundles course booking, a video channel and the training of new instructors.
buggyfit.tasks:
Takeover and ongoing development of the platform
Maintenance, bug fixes and support
buggyfit.stack: PHP, Laravel, Laravel Nova, MySQL, Redis, Vue 3, TypeScript

refs.title: Voices from projects
refs.lede: Four reviews from real collaboration, all approved.

patrick.name: Patrick Kuhlmann
patrick.text: David has been successfully taking care of the frontend and backend of my websites for over five years. He brings a lot of experience, and it shows in the high quality! What stands out most is his reliability and availability. Even on weekends he steps in on short notice when something important needs to be done.

ekkehard.name: Ekkehard Gümbel
ekkehard.text: David supported us as a TYPO3 agency in developing and maintaining our client projects. He got up to speed with our tech stack very quickly and was a great addition to our development team. His deep expertise was reflected in the quality of his work.

frederik.name: Frederik Frey
frederik.text: As an IT company, our biggest challenge is finding reliable developers who meet our quality standards. David delivers his tasks within deadlines and takes feedback to heart. When difficulties arise, he reaches out proactively and independently proposes constructive solutions.

felix.name: Felix Hahmann
felix.text: David actively supported XAIN's early phase with his web development expertise. Beyond our own web presence, he held a technical lead role in client projects, which he carried out with great dedication and professionalism paired with excellent expertise.
